rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARILYN ANN MALLEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Terminating Estate And For Discharge Of Personal Representative
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The trustees of the decedent's trust petitioned for termination of this probate and discharge of the personal representative under Probate Code section 12251. Section 12251 provides in relevant part that: "if it appears there is no property of any kind belonging to the estate and subject to administration, the personal representative may petition for the termination of further proceedings and for discharge of the personal representative." In the alternative, the petitioners request an order under section 9613 directing the personal representative to delete 50% ownership of real property commonly known as 384 Baltimore Way, San Francisco, from the inventory and appraisal. Section 9613(a) provides that: "On petition of any interested person, and upon a showing that if the petition is not granted the estate will suffer great or irreparable injury, the court may direct the personal representative to act or not to act concerning the estate. The order may include terms and conditions the court determines are appropriate under the circumstances." PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF THOMAS IVELI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/29/21 . (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: As previously noted in the tentative ruling for 7/19/21, the Third Amended I&A filed on 2/4/21 changed the assets on hand as of 11/14/19 to $2,448,039.31. The second amended account attached to the Notice of Errata filed on 1/28/21 begins with assets totaling $2,964,496.15. (The second amended petition filed on 2/1/21 (for a hearing on 12/22/20?) did not have any exhibits attached to it, although that was in fact the most recent filed pleading, superseding the "Notice of Errata" filed on 1/28/21. [CRC 7.3(3).]) The accounting attached to the second amended petition filed on 12/14/20 attached to the "Notice of Errata" filed on 1/28/21 was for the period 11/4/19 through 1/23/20. The temporary co-conservators were appointed on 11/14/19, which should be the beginning date of the accounting. The temporary conservatee died on 12/2/19, which should be the ending date of the accounting. Following the death of a conservatee, there should be a separate supplemental accounting. [Probate Code Section 2620(b).] The mandatory JC form GC-400(SUM)/GC-405(SUM) (Summary of Account) is missing. Bank statements in conservatorships should be filed in the Confidential file, not via a declaration by the fiduciaries' attorney. The Citibank account xx2473 balance shown in the third amended I&A is the balance shown in the bank statement as of 10/1/19, not 11/14/19. The Citibank account xx6593 statement shows the balance on 11/12/19 to be $256,880.90, not $109,477.36 as shown in the third amended I&A. The First Foundation Bank account xx0448 statement shows a balance of $15,452.48 as of 1/1/20 and <$1,326.09> as of 1/31/20, not $573.04 shown to be on hand as of 1/23/20 on Schedule E. No reconciliation is shown. [LRSF 14.35 D.] Schedule E of the second amended accounting also shows $448,207.76 on hand in First Foundation Bank account xx1198, but this account was not included in the third amended I&A. The attorney's "Supplemental Declaration" filed on 7/7/21 and again on 7/16/21 AMENDED the request for fees. An amendment (by any other name) requires the same notice as the pleading it amends [CRC 7.53(a)] and, as also noted before, the only proper form of notice is the mandatory Judicial Council form GC-020 (Notice of Hearing), on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. If a pleading form of proof of service is used, the copy of the mandatory Notice of Hearing that was served must be attached to that proof of service. Courtesy copies are endorsed filed copies. Unfiled copies of pleadings will not be reviewed. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.7 B. and C.] A stack of papers with a purported index that did not, however, correspond in any order to the documents in the stack was delivered to the Examiner on 8/11/21. Documents pertaining to the motion did not need to be provided: what is currently before the Court is the temporary co-conservators' second amended accounting and Ms. Colarusso's objection to the I&A. (She does not appear to have objected to this third amended I&A filed on 2/4/21.) Courtesy copies of the Third Amended First and Final Account (which should include every pertinent exhibit and the full amount of fees currently being requested) must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/20/21. If the complete third amended account cannot be prepared in time to give statutory notice before 9/29/21, Counsel should contact the assigned Examiner to request additional time. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HOOVER LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The decedent had no children, and his parents predeceased him. The only next of kin identified by the petitioner is one first cousin in San Francisco, to whom notice of this petition appears to have been given. The petitioner's attorney has performed "free google and paid BeenVerified searches", but has been unable to find any living relatives of the decedent. 2) No one appears to have knowledge of circumstances surrounding the execution and custody of the original will, or the decedent's competency until his death. 3) It is unclear whether the decedent's registered domestic partner has sufficient capacity to understand this proceeding. If his capacity may be an issue, a guardian ad litem may be required to assess whether a spousal property petition should be filed or whether a Probate Code section 13502 election should be made. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. the court to determine whether to grant the petition and all relief as requested. appearance of both petitioners and attorney is required. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHUNG-DOW CHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: continued to 9/13/21 to address remaining issues: no proof of publication on file; no confidential attachment to the duties and liabilities form on file; no proposed order delivered to the examiner as required by local rules; no waivers of bond on form DE-142 on file (examiner notes the one page document that was attached to the petition as waivers of bond was also attached to the supplement filed 8/9/21, but as noted, the judicial council form is mandatory for waivers of bond). new deadline for curing all issues is noon on 9/3/21. please note-->examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GRANDVEL ANDREW JACKSON IRREVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Appoint Successor Co-Trustees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/29/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: The order signed and filed on 8/28/2006 in case PCN-01-281686 (Conservatorship of Cora Mary Sutton Jackson) terminated Court jurisdiction of this trust. It is not clear that this Court has jurisdiction over this trust or that this is the proper venue. The reference in paragraph 27 of the petition to mini-minutes for a hearing regarding another trust is not legal authority. Notice was mailed to Theodore Bennett and to the NAACP at addresses other than shown in the petition. The petition caption fails to disclose all relief sought. [CRC 7.102.] Were the distributions to individual beneficiaries not already fully satisfied? The issue of predeceased beneficiaries are not necessarily interested parties, as the trust does not appear to provide for issue to take other than the named alternate beneficiaries for Lillian Sutton Taylor and Lura Sims. Lillian Sutton Taylor or her successor-in-interest Alexander Carver Sutton, Sr., both predeceased the surviving settlor. (Are the issue of predeceased beneficiaries potential recipients of trust funds for student?) The prayer of this petition refers to "Respondent" but the relief is sought by the Petitioner and there has been response or objection filed. (It is also noted that the Estate of Cora Mary Sutton Jackson (PES-06-288396) remains open. That estate must now be formally closed, as Letters were issued.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/20/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RICHARD L. TRACY AND BARBARA M. TRACY DECLARATION OF TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Compel Trustee To Account And Distribute Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/27/2021. Notes were e-mailed to counsel on 08/18/2021. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD MAHLON FRAZER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/20/21. No courtesy copies. No proposed order. No proof of publication. No Duties and Liabilities. No Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License (filed confidentially). A quick skim of the petition reflects only maternal heirs listed on Attachment 8, no paternal heirs. Courtesy copies of the petition and all related papers and a proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/10/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
